questions.question: Which supplier provides the Frame, and what is its lead time and unit cost?
reference_key_facts: Frames Ltd supplies the Frame
14 day lead time
$120 unit cost

questions.question: Which supplier provides the Suspension Fork, and what is its lead time and unit cost?
reference_key_facts: Forks Co supplies the Suspension Fork
12 day lead time
$150 unit cost, the most expensive single part PedalWorks buys

questions.question: What numeric stock threshold does the Planner use to decide a part needs reordering, and which file defines that value?
reference_key_facts: the threshold value is 1
defined as _LOW_STOCK_THRESHOLD in src/planner.py
questions.notes: docs/inventory.md and docs/planning_and_demand.md only describe the low-stock rule in prose without a number; the actual value lives only in src/planner.py, so this is a provenance question.

questions.question: Which file defines the LedgerEntry dataclass, and what are its three fields?
reference_key_facts: src/models.py defines LedgerEntry
fields are stage, kind, and amount

questions.question: What happens to the very first bicycle inspected in a PedalWorks production run?
reference_key_facts: it gets a first-article inspection
it always fails
it returns to Assembly for rework and is inspected again
questions.notes: docs/production.md describes the general rework loop but never states that the first unit always fails; that specific rule is only in src/quality.py's docstring and its `_inspected == 1` code path.

questions.question: Walk me through what happens to one City Cruiser order, from supplier parts all the way to shipment.
reference_key_facts: the Planner triggers Procurement to reorder a part when PartsDB shows it low
Assembly builds the City Cruiser from a Frame, Wheelset, Drivetrain, and Brake Set
Quality inspects the unit and reworks failures via Assembly before re-inspecting
Shipping sends the finished bicycle out from the South Distribution Center and marks the order shipped in OrderDB

questions.question: I'm new here -- can you explain PedalWorks' two 'hub' roles, the Planner and the Ledger, and how they differ?
reference_key_facts: the Planner is the fan-out hub: it reads OrderDB and PartsDB, triggers Procurement on low stock, and releases work to Assembly
the Ledger is the fan-in hub: many processes each post one cost to it
the Ledger is persisted in LedgerDB

questions.question: For accounting purposes, walk me through every point in the factory where money gets spent, from raw part purchase to final shipment.
reference_key_facts: Procurement posts a Purchase Cost each time it orders parts from a supplier
Assembly posts a Labor Cost each time it puts a unit together, and posts another Labor Cost each time it reworks a unit that failed Quality
Quality posts a Scrap Cost each time it runs an inspection, whether the unit passes or fails
Shipping posts a Freight Cost each time it ships a finished unit out
all four postings land in the shared Ledger, backed by LedgerDB

questions.question: How does building a Trail Blazer differ from building a City Cruiser, from parts sourcing through assembly?
reference_key_facts: the City Cruiser is built from four parts: a Frame, a Wheelset, a Drivetrain, and a Brake Set
the Trail Blazer shares two of those three -- the Frame and the Wheelset -- but swaps out the Drivetrain and Brake Set for a Suspension Fork instead
every City Cruiser and every Trail Blazer goes through the same five Assembly steps and the same Quality inspection

questions.question: What are PedalWorks' three named data systems, and which stages read or write each one?
reference_key_facts: PartsDB holds part stock levels, read and written by Inventory, the Planner, and Procurement
OrderDB holds customer orders, read by the Planner to find open orders and updated by Shipping when an order is marked shipped
LedgerDB is the backing store for the Ledger, holding every posted LedgerEntry

questions.question: Trace how the Planner noticing low stock eventually turns into an entry in the Ledger.
reference_key_facts: the Planner triggers Procurement to place an order when a part's stock has run low
Procurement posts that purchase cost to the Ledger

questions.question: When a unit fails Quality and is sent back for rework, how does that eventually show up in the Ledger?
reference_key_facts: a unit that fails Quality returns to Assembly for rework rather than being scrapped
Assembly re-posts a labor cost for the extra work before Quality inspects it again

questions.question: Quality and Shipping are different stages, but they both feed the same downstream record. Which one, and what does each stage post to it?
reference_key_facts: Quality posts a scrap cost to the Ledger
Shipping posts a freight cost to the Ledger
both postings converge in the same Ledger, backed by LedgerDB

questions.question: The Frame feeds into both PedalWorks bicycle models. Which supplier provides it, and which second model besides the City Cruiser uses it?
reference_key_facts: Frames Ltd supplies the Frame
the Trail Blazer shares the Frame with the City Cruiser

questions.question: What material is the Frame of a City Cruiser made of?
reference_key_facts: the City Cruiser is built from a Frame
the Frame is made of Aluminium

questions.chain: Forks Co --supplies--> Suspension Fork --used_in--> Trail Blazer <--produces-- Assembly
questions.question: Trace the Suspension Fork from its supplier to the finished bicycle: who supplies it, which model uses it, and which stage produces that model?
reference_key_facts: Forks Co supplies the Suspension Fork
the Trail Blazer swaps in the Suspension Fork instead of the Drivetrain and Brake Set
Trail Blazer stage builds the Trail Blazer on the Assembly Floor

questions.question: According to the supplier catalog document, what region is Forks Co based in?
reference_key_facts: Forks Co is based in the North-East region
questions.notes: Image-only fact: Forks Co's region is stated only inside a PIL-drawn 'Supplier Region Note' image embedded in the PDF (kb-test-tooling/gen_binary_fixtures.py REGION_FACT_VALUE); docs/procurement.md gives every other supplier's region in prose but omits Forks Co's on purpose. Recoverable now via the vision-description step the extractor runs over embedded images, so this is answerable.

questions.question: In the quality checklist document's table, what standard and pass threshold does the Brake Test check against?
reference_key_facts: Lever force test standard
Holds at 800 N, no pad slip
questions.notes: The QC-check standard and pass threshold exist only inside the docx table; docs/production.md says the checks are 'documented separately in the QC checklist' but never states an individual standard or threshold.

questions.question: According to the inventory stock spreadsheet, how many units of the Suspension Fork are currently on hand, and what is its reorder threshold?
reference_key_facts: 4 units on hand
reorder threshold of 3
questions.notes: These figures exist only in the xlsx stock table; docs/inventory.md discusses stock tracking generically with no per-part numbers.

questions.question: What retail price does PedalWorks charge a customer for a City Cruiser?
questions.notes: The corpus only records internal costs (supplier unit_cost, and labor/scrap/freight postings to the Ledger); no customer-facing sale price is ever stated anywhere in docs/, src/, or the binary fixtures. Tests refusal.

questions.question: What is PedalWorks' warranty policy for a Trail Blazer sold to a customer?
questions.notes: Warranty/customer-policy content is out of scope -- the corpus covers internal factory operations (planning, supply, inventory, production, fulfillment, finance) only, with no such content anywhere. Tests refusal.

questions.question: What material is the Suspension Fork made of?
reference_key_facts: the Suspension Fork is made of Carbon Fibre

questions.question: What are the sub-parts that make up a Wheelset?
reference_key_facts: a Wheelset is made up of Rims, Spokes, a Hub, and Tyres

questions.question: List the sub-parts of the Drivetrain.
reference_key_facts: the Drivetrain's sub-parts are the Chain, Cassette, Crankset, and Derailleur

questions.question: In the Assembly build sequence, which step immediately follows Mount Frame?
reference_key_facts: Attach Wheelset immediately follows Mount Frame
the full order is Mount Frame -> Attach Wheelset -> Install Drivetrain -> Fit Brakes -> Final Fit

questions.question: Which stage posts the Scrap Cost, and where does it post it?
reference_key_facts: Quality posts the Scrap Cost
it posts to the Ledger

questions.question: What kind of cost does a Purchase Ledger Entry record?
reference_key_facts: a Purchase Ledger Entry is of kind Purchase Cost

questions.question: Which stage reads OrderDB to find open orders, and which stage updates it when an order ships?
reference_key_facts: the Planner reads OrderDB to find open orders
Shipping updates OrderDB when an order is marked shipped

questions.question: Which parts is the Trail Blazer built from?
reference_key_facts: the Trail Blazer is built from a Frame, a Wheelset, and a Suspension Fork

questions.question: From which location does Shipping send finished bicycles out?
reference_key_facts: Shipping sends finished bicycles from the South Distribution Center

questions.question: According to the source code, on which floor does the Trail Blazer stage build the Trail Blazer?
reference_key_facts: the Trail Blazer is built on the Assembly Floor
questions.notes: Code-provenance split: the 'Assembly Floor' location string lives in src/trailblazer.py, not in prose.

questions.question: According to the factory overview slides, what is the stated capacity of the North Intake Warehouse?
reference_key_facts: 3000 units
questions.notes: PPTX-only: facility capacity figures live only inside docs/factory_overview.pptx slides; no prose doc states them.

questions.question: In the yield analysis notebook, what first-pass yield does PedalWorks compute?
questions.notes: Notebook-only: First-Pass Yield is derived and stated only in analysis/yield_analysis.ipynb; exercises the notebook preprocessing lane.

questions.question: Explain how PedalWorks handles a bicycle that fails inspection.
reference_key_facts: a unit that fails Quality is sent back to Assembly for rework rather than scrapped
Assembly re-posts a Labor Cost for the rework
Quality re-inspects the reworked unit
Quality posts a Scrap Cost for each inspection to the Ledger

questions.question: Walk me through how PedalWorks keeps parts in stock and decides when to reorder.
reference_key_facts: Stock Tracking monitors PartsDB for stock levels
the Planner reads PartsDB and detects when a part has run low
the Planner triggers Procurement to reorder from the part's supplier

questions.question: Give me a bill-of-materials overview: what are the five parts and the material each is made of?
reference_key_facts: Frame is made of Aluminium
Wheelset is made of Alloy
Drivetrain is made of Steel
Brake Set is made of Steel & Rubber
Suspension Fork is made of Carbon Fibre

questions.question: Summarize PedalWorks' supply chain: which supplier provides which part?
reference_key_facts: Frames Ltd supplies the Frame
Wheels Co supplies the Wheelset
Drivetrain Co supplies the Drivetrain
Brakes Ltd supplies the Brake Set
Forks Co supplies the Suspension Fork

questions.chain: City Cruiser --uses--> Brake Set --made_of--> Steel & Rubber
questions.question: The City Cruiser uses a Brake Set. What material is that Brake Set made of?
reference_key_facts: the City Cruiser uses a Brake Set
the Brake Set is made of Steel & Rubber

questions.chain: Trail Blazer --uses--> Suspension Fork <--supplies-- Forks Co --region--> North-East

reference_key_facts: the Trail Blazer uses the Suspension Fork
Forks Co supplies the Suspension Fork
Forks Co is based in the North-East region
questions.notes: Cross-format hard-band chain: model->part->supplier from prose, supplier->region from the PDF embedded image (recovered by the vision extractor).

questions.chain: Suspension Fork Stock --tracks--> Suspension Fork <--supplies-- Forks Co
questions.question: The inventory spreadsheet tracks a low Suspension Fork stock. When the Planner reorders it, which supplier receives the order?
reference_key_facts: the Suspension Fork Stock row tracks the Suspension Fork
Forks Co supplies the Suspension Fork, so Procurement orders from Forks Co
questions.notes: Cross-format chain: xlsx stock row -> part -> supplier (prose).

questions.question: How long does PedalWorks take to deliver a finished bicycle to a customer's home address?
questions.notes: Plausible-but-absent: the corpus covers internal fulfillment (Shipping out of the South Distribution Center) but never a customer delivery time/SLA. Tests refusal.

questions.question: How many workers does PedalWorks employ on the Assembly floor?
questions.notes: Plausible-but-absent: the corpus describes Assembly as a process with build steps and labor cost, but never any headcount/staffing figure anywhere. Tests refusal.
